atoms.source_snippet: pub max_block_size_bytes: Option<usize>; default None; with_max_block_size_bytes(...)
atoms.semantic_claim: EthereumBuilderConfig carries an optional max_block_size_bytes limit, defaults it to None, and exposes a fluent setter so payload construction can enforce a configured maximum block-size budget.

destinations.snippet: pub max_block_size_bytes: Option<usize>; ... pub const fn with_max_block_size_bytes(mut self, max_block_size_bytes: Option<usize>) -> Self
destinations.evidence: The destination config carries the optional byte limit, defaults it to None, and exposes the fluent setter.
Payload construction consumes the configured limit while preserving the Osaka MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE cap.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Configure a small builder max block size and assert payload building skips transactions that would exceed it while still sealing a valid payload.
atoms.notes: Resolved by ba3727354e; node-level forwarding is tracked in file-0084.

atoms.source_snippet: DEFAULT_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS = 300; pub build_deadline: Duration; RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS; with_build_deadline(...)
atoms.semantic_claim: EthereumBuilderConfig carries a per-build wall-clock deadline, reads RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS at construction time, falls back to 300ms when unset or invalid, and exposes a setter for explicit deadline overrides.

destinations.snippet: const DEFAULT_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS: u64 = 300; ... std::env::var("RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS") ... build_deadline: Duration::from_millis(build_deadline_ms)
destinations.evidence: The destination config reads RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS, falls back to 300ms, stores build_deadline, and exposes the explicit setter.

destinations.snippet: let global_deadline = build_start + builder_config.build_deadline; ... build deadline reached, sealing block with already executed transactions
destinations.evidence: The per-build deadline controls sequencing reorder and transaction execution loop partial sealing behavior.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Set RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS=0 and assert payload building exits transaction execution quickly and seals whatever has already been processed instead of running the full loop.
atoms.notes: Current destination has the runtime deadline behavior; it is not merely job-level max duration.

atoms.source_snippet: pub fn new(); pub fn with_gas_limit(...); pub fn with_await_payload_on_missing(...)
atoms.semantic_claim: EthereumBuilderConfig::new and existing fluent setters become non-const runtime functions after adding environment-dependent deadline construction and runtime-only configuration fields.

destinations.snippet: pub fn new() -> Self { ... } ... pub const fn with_gas_limit(...) ... pub const fn with_await_payload_on_missing(...)
destinations.evidence: The destination makes new() runtime-dependent as required by env parsing while keeping pure fluent setters const where possible.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The behavior-bearing API change is that new() can no longer be const because it reads the environment. Keeping pure setters const is a compatible stricter/superset API shape and does not remove the runtime config behavior.
atoms.notes: Non-exact API shape only; no behavior gap remains.

atoms.source_snippet: tests for default/env/invalid build_deadline, explicit override, builder chaining, zero/large deadlines, clone preservation, and gas-limit calculation
atoms.semantic_claim: Unit tests cover build deadline defaults, env parsing, invalid fallback, explicit overrides, builder chaining with max block size, zero and large deadlines, clone preservation, and gas-limit calculation invariants.

destinations.evidence: The destination has focused tests for env/default/invalid deadline behavior, explicit overrides, max block size setter, builder chaining, and gas-limit invariants.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Port the runtime fields and add serial/env-locked tests for RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS behavior.
atoms.notes: Resolved by current destination tests, including max_block_size_bytes coverage added in ba3727354e.

summary: EthereumBuilderConfig now contains max_block_size_bytes and build_deadline behavior with regression tests; only the low-risk const-API shape differs from the old downstream source.
notes: Updated after ba3727354e: current config.rs has max_block_size_bytes, RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS parsing, with_build_deadline, with_max_block_size_bytes, and focused tests.
